graded_note: 4 researched input fields per app (auth, access, API surface, MCP) × 20 apps. Buildability is derived from these via the score, so it is not graded separately.
explain: An independent agent — a different, stronger model with an adversarial "assume this is wrong" prompt — re-researched the 20-app sample from scratch and was diffed against the first pass. Raw agreement was 73% across 80 graded input fields. Adjudicating every disagreement against primary docs by hand: 9 were genuine first-pass errors (now fixed), 3 were the auditor's own mistakes (first pass upheld), and 10 were vocabulary refinements. Re-graded against the docs, the corrected sample sits at 96% — the rest is left honest for the genuinely ambiguous sandbox / enterprise edges. Separately, every one of the 100 evidence links was fetched: 99% resolved live.

diffs.why: First pass counted a speculative, not-yet-shipped MCP as official.
Slack shipped a first-party MCP server (GA Feb 2026) — first pass missed it.
Smartsheet ships an official MCP server; first pass hedged to unknown.
A real (Enterprise-gated) lookup API exists — 'no public API' was too strong.
Follows from the above: there is a REST surface, just paywalled.
Clay ships an official MCP server.
Firecrawl is API-key only; OAuth2 was an over-claim.
A public Higgsfield Cloud REST API exists beyond the CLI.
Bearer API key only; OAuth2 over-claimed.
Auditor wrong — API keys need a paid Aircall subscription. First pass upheld.
Auditor wrong — Stripe live keys are self-serve on activation, no gate. First pass upheld.
Auditor wrong — Smartsheet API needs a paid/trial plan, no durable free tier. First pass upheld.
Both mean gated; 'gated-partner' (contact-sales, no signup) is more precise. Adopted.
Community SP-API MCP servers exist. Adopted.
JWT bearer flow is a real Salesforce auth path; tag completed.
GitHub App auth uses a JWT; tag completed.
